{"file_path":"/home/runner/work/Kody-Engine-Tester/Kody-Engine-Tester/.kody/tasks/2335-260417-190801/plan.md","content":"# Plan: Add/Update User Payload Collection with Password Hash Field

## Context

The codebase has dual auth systems (`UserStore` with SHA-256 vs `AuthService` with PBKDF2/JWT) creating inconsistency. `src/collections/Users.ts` already exists with `auth: true` (Payload built-in auth), and `src/auth/auth-service.ts` already accesses `(user as any).hash` and `(user as any).salt`. However, the `Users` collection lacks an explicit unique index on `email`, and no migration script exists. The task also needs the collection to be clearly documented as using Payload's built-in PBKDF2 password field (named `password` ‚Üí Payload auto-manages `hash` + `salt` internally).

## Scope

### Files to modify:
- `src/collections/Users.ts` ‚Äî add unique `email` index, add explicit `hash`/`salt` fields to match auth-service usage
- `src/collections/Users.test.ts` ‚Äî add tests for e‚Ä¶ [+3176 chars]

Should the `hash`/`salt` fields be added explicitly to the collection (for auth-service.ts which reads them), or should auth-service.ts instead call Payload's `verifyPassword` API? These are two different approaches with different trade-offs.","header":"Hash strategy","options":[{"label":"Add hash+salt fields to Users collection","description":"Add explicit hidden hash/salt text fields to Users.ts. auth-service.ts already reads them ‚Äî no changes to auth logic needed."},{"label":"Switch auth-service to use Payload's verifyPassword API","description":"Keep Users.ts using Payload's built-in password field.
